question: What is the main objective of the Manufacturing Quality Induction Module?
options: A. To teach machinery operation techniques
B. To familiarize employees with the importance of quality in manufacturing
C. To train workers in accounting and logistics

question: Which statement best defines Manufacturing?
options: A. Trading goods between suppliers and customers
B. Providing repair and maintenance services
C. Transforming raw materials or components into finished goods using machines, tools, labour, and technology
D. Managing warehouse and logistics operations

question: Which of the following is an example of Discrete Manufacturing?
options: C. Production of automobiles and machinery

question: What does Quality in Manufacturing primarily ensure?
options: B. Defect-free, reliable, durable, and safe products that meet or exceed customer expectations

question: Which of the following is a key aspect of quality?
options: B. Conformance to specifications and standards

question: What is the main focus of Quality Assurance (QA)?
options: B. Preventing defects by improving processes

question: Which international standard focuses on Quality Management Systems (QMS)?
The IATF 16949 standard is specific to which industry?
What is the purpose of Continuous Improvement in manufacturing?
options: B. To reduce waste and improve efficiency using methods like Kaizen and Six Sigma
D. To replace workers with machines

question: Which of the following best describes the role of employee training and awareness in assuring product quality?
options: A. It helps reduce the number of machines used
B. It ensures accountability, process discipline, and consistent quality performance
C. It replaces the need for inspection

question: What is the main purpose of Inhouse Quality Systems in manufacturing?
options: B. To ensure every product meets defined quality standards through systematic controls
C. To focus only on supplier inspections
D. To manage logistics and dispatch operations

question: Which of the following is NOT one of the three main Inhouse Quality Systems?
What is the main function of the Incoming Quality System (IQS)?
options: B. To inspect and verify quality of raw materials and components received from suppliers
D. To monitor energy consumption in the plant

question: The In-Process Quality System (PQS) helps to:
options: A. Inspect finished goods before dispatch
B. Maintain quality during production and detect defects early

question: What is the role of a Final Quality System (FQS)?
options: B. To verify that finished products conform to all specifications before dispatch
C. To record machine maintenance schedules

question: What does an SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) ensure on the shopfloor?
options: B. Consistency, safety, and compliance in performing tasks

question: What is an Inspection Standard Sheet (ISS) used for?
options: A. To define inspection criteria, methods, frequency, and acceptance standards
C. To record attendance of inspectors

question: In manufacturing, a Defect is defined as:
options: A. Any product that is delayed in delivery
B. Any nonconformance or deviation from specified requirements
D. A minor visual imperfection that doesn’t affect function

question: What does PPM (Parts Per Million) indicate?
options: B. The number of defective units per one million units produced
C. The production speed of a process
D. The percentage of manpower utilization

question: The purpose of a Defect Report is to:
options: B. Document the defect, analyze its cause, and ensure corrective and preventive actions

question: What is the main objective of formal methods in ensuring product quality?
options: B. To provide a structured and repeatable approach to meet customer expectations and standards

question: What is the primary difference between Quality Control (QC) and Quality Assurance (QA)?
options: A. QC focuses on preventing defects, QA on detecting them
B. QA focuses on preventing defects, QC on detecting them
C. QC and QA mean the same
D. QA involves only final inspection

question: Which of the following activities is associated with Quality Control (QC)?
options: B. In-process inspection and final inspection

question: Which of the following is an example of Quality Assurance (QA) activity?
options: A. Checking dimensions of a finished part
B. Performing process audits and ensuring calibration of measuring instruments

question: The main purpose of Quality Inspection is to:
options: A. Evaluate product performance under load
B. Detect nonconformities before the product moves to the next stage

question: Quality Testing primarily focuses on:
options: B. Verifying product performance and functionality under specified conditions

question: Which of the following statements about Destructive Testing (DT) is TRUE?
options: A. The sample remains intact after testing
B. It is mainly used for process quality control during production
C. It destroys the sample to measure properties like strength and durability
D. It is faster than Non-Destructive Testing

question: What does Non-Destructive Testing (NDT) help determine?
options: B. Internal and surface defects without damaging the product

question: The purpose of Boundary Samples is to:
options: A. Define the range of acceptable visual quality standards for inspection

question: The difference between 100% Inspection and Sampling Inspection is that:
options: A. Both inspect every item produced
B. 100% checks all items, while Sampling checks only selected samples from the batch
C. Sampling is slower and more expensive

question: What is the main purpose of defining Quality Targets in manufacturing?
options: B. To ensure products consistently meet customer expectations and quality standards

question: Which of the following is NOT a reason for defining Quality Targets?
options: B. To monitor performance and compliance

question: Quality targets are generally set using which SMART criteria?
options: A. Safe, Measurable, Applicable, Reliable, Time-bound
B. Simple, Manageable, Accurate, Rational, Timely
C. Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound
D. Sustainable, Meaningful, Accountable, Realistic, Trackable

question: What does Process Quality in manufacturing refer to?
options: A. The appearance of the final product
B. The ability of a process to consistently produce products that meet specifications
D. The quality of marketing materials

question: Which parameter measures how well a process performs within specification limits?
Which of the following is NOT typically included in a Quality Performance Card (QPC)?
What does a Customer PPM Requirement represent?
options: A. The number of spare parts supplied per million
B. The maximum allowable number of defective parts per million units delivered
C. The total number of products produced in a month
D. The process cycle time per product

question: If a customer specifies a PPM requirement of ≤500, what does it mean?
options: A. No more than 500 defective parts per one million supplied
B. The supplier must produce 500 parts per minute
C. The supplier must ship exactly 500 parts per batch
D. The customer expects 500% production efficiency

question: What is the main difference between Daily QPC and Monthly QPC?
options: A. Daily QPC is for management review; Monthly QPC is for operators
B. Daily QPC tracks immediate performance; Monthly QPC shows long-term trends
C. Monthly QPC is used on the shopfloor only

question: What happens when the Customer PPM target is not met?
options: A. The customer may apply penalties or request corrective actions
C. The supplier ignores the target
D. The customer reduces their PPM requirement

question: What is the primary role of qualified people in the quality function of manufacturing?
options: A. To reduce manpower in production
B. To ensure products meet design specifications, performance standards, and customer expectations
C. To focus only on marketing and sales
D. To manage raw material procurement

question: Which of the following is a key contribution of qualified quality professionals?
options: C. Identifying potential sources of variation and implementing preventive actions

question: The Chief Quality Officer (CQO) is responsible for:
options: A. Operating machines on the shopfloor
B. Defining the company’s Quality Vision, Policy, and Objectives

question: The Quality Manager / Plant Quality Head primarily:
options: A. Manages the marketing and sales team
B. Leads the Quality Department at the plant level, overseeing QA, QC, and audits
C. Designs new products for R&D

question: The Quality Control (QC) Engineer / Inspector is mainly responsible for:
options: B. Performing in-process and final inspections using measuring instruments

question: The Supplier Quality Engineer (SQE) focuses on:
options: B. Ensuring supplier quality performance and conducting supplier audits

question: The Customer Quality Engineer (CQE) handles:
options: B. Customer complaints, field failures, and Corrective Action (8D) reports

question: The Metrology Engineer / Calibration Technician ensures:
options: C. Measuring instruments and gauges are calibrated and accurate
D. Final products are packaged correctly

question: The Continuous Improvement / Six Sigma Engineer is responsible for:
options: A. Identifying and implementing process and quality improvement projects using Lean or Six Sigma

question: Which statement best defines the role of every employee in quality?
options: A. Only the quality department is responsible for product quality
B. Every employee is a quality ambassador responsible for process discipline and continuous improvement
C. Quality is ensured only through management audits
D. Quality responsibility ends with the final inspector
